_comment: Golden-template Project VIEW CATALOG (gh-projects, Phase 1). The 8 saved views are built BY HAND once on the golden-template Project (Phase 0) and replicate via copyProjectV2 — ProjectV2 views are NOT API-mutable (no create/edit). They ARE, however, API-READABLE (the projectV2.views connection: name/layout/filter/groupByFields/verticalGroupByFields), so scaffold-repo verifies the copy carries all 8 by name (AC-7, the 'view catalog' half) AND that each view RESOLVES its documented filter/group/slice without error (AC-25, §6 verify_views). A missing view or an unresolved filter/group/slice is a template/copy defect: fix it on the golden template and re-copy — never hand-add to a scaffolded project (it would drift). Full per-view sort/card specs live in views.md; this file is the minimal machine-checkable catalog. Each view declares: name, layout, the expected raw `filter` string (substring qualifiers that MUST be present in the copied view's resolved filter), `group` (the field name the view groups its columns/rows by, must resolve to a real project/issue field — empty for ungrouped), and `slice` (the verticalGroupBy / slice-panel field name, must resolve to a real field — empty when the view has no slice). Resolution = the field referenced by group/slice exists on the copy (re-resolved from the COPY, never the template) and every filter qualifier the catalog declares is present in the live view's filter string.

_field_qualifiers._comment: Maps a filter qualifier KEYWORD (left of the colon in a saved-search filter, e.g. `status:Ready`) to the project/issue FIELD NAME it resolves against. A declared filter qualifier 'resolves' when its keyword maps here AND the mapped field exists on the copy. Native GitHub qualifiers (is, assignee, no) need no field and are listed as native.
